eula.title: Sepsis Vitals
eula.subtitle: End User License Agreement
eula.criticalBody: Sepsis Vitals is a research-grade investigational tool. It is NOT cleared, approved, or authorized by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) or any other regulatory body for autonomous clinical decision-making, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.
eula.section1Body: Sepsis Vitals ("the Software") is designed solely for research, educational, and investigational purposes. It provides clinical decision support based on vital-sign analysis and machine learning models. It must not be used as the sole or primary basis for any clinical decision, diagnosis, or treatment plan.
eula.section2Body: The Software does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Any predictions, risk scores, or alerts generated by the Software are probabilistic estimates intended to supplement — never replace — clinical judgment.
eula.section2Bold: All clinical decisions and medical liability rest solely with the attending physician or licensed healthcare provider.
eula.section3Rest: . It has not been submitted to, reviewed by, or cleared/approved by the FDA, CE, or any other regulatory authority. No clinical validation study has been completed to support its use in patient care.
eula.section4Body: The machine learning models within Sepsis Vitals were trained on synthetic data calibrated to population distributions. They have not been validated on real patient data or in a clinical setting. Model performance metrics (e.g., AUROC, sensitivity, specificity) reflect performance on synthetic test sets and should not be interpreted as clinically validated performance.
eula.section5Body: IN NO EVENT SHALL SEPSIS VITALS, INC., ITS FOUNDERS, DEVELOPERS, OR AFFILIATES BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING FROM THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MISDIAGNOSIS, DELAYED TREATMENT, OR PATIENT HARM.
eula.section6Body: The Software is designed with HIPAA-compliant architecture including AES-256-GCM encryption at rest, TLS in transit, role-based access control, and audit logging. However, compliance certification has not been independently audited. Users are responsible for ensuring their deployment meets applicable privacy regulations.
eula.section7Body: By clicking "I Agree" below, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by this End User License Agreement. If you do not agree, you may not use the Software.
eula.version: EULA Version 1.0.0 — Effective June 2026 — Sepsis Vitals, Inc.
eula.scrollHint: Scroll to the bottom of the agreement to continue
eula.checkboxLabel: I have read and understand that Sepsis Vitals is a research-grade tool, is not FDA-cleared, and that all medical liability rests with the attending physician.
eula.footerNote: This agreement is required each time the EULA version is updated.
